title: Rewrite non-tail recursion to use an accumulator
prompt: Refactor `MyApp.ListUtils.doubled/1` in test_fixtures/non_tail_recursive.ex. The original calls `[head * 2] ++ doubled(tail)` — not tail-recursive (the `++` wraps the recursive call). Rewrite as a tail-recursive version that passes an accumulator, prepending with `[head * 2 | acc]`, and reverses at the end. Use a public `doubled/1` that calls a private `do_doubled/2`. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.ListUtils do ... end` with only `doubled/1` and its helper (drop the other functions).
tier_rationale: Canonical recursion transformation. Two-step (drop ++, add accumulator helper). Sonnet often writes the prepend form but forgets the reverse.
